mechanism: Broad tyrosine kinase inhibition (including some activity vs FLT3/BCR‑ABL depending on context), blocking oncogenic kinase signaling
additional_notes: Reported preclinical activity in AML models. No concentrations provided in source text.

mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase, induces oxidative stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Gold compound with reported cytotoxicity in AML cell lines and primary blasts. No concentrations provided in source text.

mechanism: Inhibits ALDH and, when combined with copper, generates reactive species that induce cancer cell death
additional_notes: Often studied with copper for anticancer effects. No concentrations provided in source text.

mechanism: Disrupts microtubules and other cancer‑relevant processes, leading to impaired mitosis and cell death
additional_notes: Repurposing studies show activity in hematologic malignancies. No concentrations provided in source text.

mechanism: Reported multimodal anticancer actions (mitochondrial uncoupling, Wnt/STAT3 inhibition, etc.) leading to reduced proliferation
additional_notes: Multiple proposed mechanisms; preclinical activity reported in some AML models. No concentrations provided in source text.

mechanism: Inhibits HMG‑CoA reductase, affecting cholesterol synthesis and prenylation pathways that can influence apoptosis and chemosensitivity
additional_notes: Listed as example of statins; reported to induce apoptosis and enhance chemo‑sensitivity in AML. No concentrations provided in source text.

mechanism: Alters cellular metabolism and AMPK signaling, which can reduce proliferation and survival in cancer cells
additional_notes: Preclinical antiproliferative effects reported in AML. No concentrations provided in source text.

mechanism: Inhibits lysosomal function/autophagy, which can sensitize leukemic cells to other treatments
additional_notes: Source lists chloroquine/hydroxychloroquine as autophagy inhibitors used in AML combination studies. No concentrations provided in source text.

mechanism: Reversible inhibition of the 26S proteasome, leading to accumulation of misfolded proteins and induction of apoptosis
additional_notes: Tested in AML clinical trials and preclinical studies. No concentrations provided in source text.

mechanism: Irreversible proteasome inhibition leading to apoptosis in proteasome‑dependent cancer cells
additional_notes: Next‑generation proteasome inhibitor with preclinical AML activity. No concentrations provided in source text.

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1/2, blocking the MAPK pathway and downstream proliferative signaling
additional_notes: Studied in combinations for AML subsets. No concentrations provided in source text.

mechanism: Inhibits Src/Abl family kinases and other tyrosine kinases, disrupting proliferative and survival signaling
additional_notes: Has activity against kinases implicated in AML biology; studied preclinically in combinations. No concentrations provided in source text.

mechanism: Inhibits Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK) and related signaling nodes, affecting survival pathways in some myeloid contexts
additional_notes: Some preclinical AML activity reported, often in combination settings. No concentrations provided in source text.

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2 kinases, suppressing JAK/STAT signaling implicated in myeloid malignancies
additional_notes: Studied in MPNs and subsets of AML/MDS; potential combination partner. No concentrations provided in source text.

mechanism: Induces apoptosis and differentiation (notably effective in APL via PML‑RARα degradation and pro‑apoptotic effects)
additional_notes: Established therapy in APL; explored preclinically in other AML subsets and combinations. No concentrations provided in source text.
